commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8ea8e88574e8e3cbf76d3f5deb6bb78ce62e2165)
commands.review_summary:
The PR decouples Codex app-server user MCP server attachment from native code-mode enablement, filters projected user MCP servers by the effective tool allowlist, emits Codex `enabled_tools` for exact tool entries, and updates Codex harness docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +132, Tests +149, Docs +19. Total +300 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level for the review findings: PR-head projection reserves disabled server names before filtering, while current allowlist context skips disabled names first, and the changed docs contradict the PR-head `enabled_tools` behavior. I did not run a live Codex app-server scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer follow-up is required because the PR changes a Codex MCP security boundary, lacks real behavior proof, and needs author-side fixes for a prefix-contract edge case plus docs accuracy.
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger
